key_insight: Parallel-route coherent mixup (PaRC-mix) improved prostate MRI lesion segmentation generalization across scanners and institutions by augmenting features at multiple network layers, not just at the input.
clinical_relevance: Better cross-domain segmentation could support more reliable MR-guided adaptive radiotherapy planning for prostate cancer, where accurate daily delineation is critical.
method: Trained MRRN and UNet++ models with PaRC-mix feature mixing at multiple layers.
Used ADC MRI to segment dominant intraprostatic lesions from 3.0T GE training data.
Compared against no-mixup, input-mixup, and backbone-only mixup baselines.
evidence_signal: Models were trained on 2,029 samples from one MRI source and tested on 1,547 samples from five external datasets spanning Siemens, Philips, and MR-Linac scanners, with PaRC-mix outperforming comparison methods on segmentation metrics and recall-precision balance.
watch_item: Results come from retrospective multi-domain testing on one lesion segmentation task, so broader clinical deployment will still need prospective validation and site-specific implementation checks.
paper_title: Enhancing Prostate Cancer Segmentation for Multi-Domain Generalization using a novel Parallel-Route Coherent Mixup Regularization Training
